title: contrib/cirrus/lib.sh: don't use CN for the hostname
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

When generating a certificate to be used by a registry server that we're bringing up, instead of setting a CN value in the subject name to "localhost" to pass the name check that a client makes, use a subject alternative name extension.

The "compare the hostname we're given with a CN from the subject name field" method stops working if the client is built with Go 1.15.

#### How to verify it

Tests which passed before should continue to pass, even when run using a binary built with Go 1.15.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
None
```

comments.body: @edsantiago, I think this'll fix an error we're hitting in Fedora's gating tests.
You beat me to it, thank you! Running it through the wringer now, with equivalent changes in gating-test scripts
For reference, https://github.com/golang/go/issues/39568#issuecomment-671424481.  The whole issue, really, but that comment has some useful advice.

LGTM. Clean run of `authenticate.bats` with this. I will push the same change on the fedpkg side. 

Thank you for jumping on this so quickly and for the helpful annotations.
